compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can find ast-grep installation and usage guidance in project docs
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add ast-grep (sg) as a recommended dependency in README.md with platform-specific installation instructions, and add CLAUDE.md guidance establishing sg as the preferred tool for structural code search with concrete example patterns.
**Why**: Agents currently rely on text-based grep which cannot distinguish structural code patterns from comments or string literals, leading to incomplete impact analysis. Documenting ast-grep as the preferred structural search tool gives agents a concrete alternative.
**Scope**:
- IN: README.md installation instructions (macOS brew, Linux cargo), CLAUDE.md section on structural code search with at least 2 example patterns (bash source chain, Python cross-file import), note about sg vs ast-grep binary naming
- OUT: Skill-level integration (separate stories), wrapper scripts, gate/hook integration, ast-grep pattern config files (belong in 1a5b-35f9 centrality epic)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, README.md contains ast-grep installation instructions for macOS (brew install ast-grep) and Linux (cargo install ast-grep --locked)
  ← Satisfies: "ast-grep is documented as a recommended dependency"
- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md contains a section establishing sg as the preferred tool for structural code search when exploring cross-file dependencies, with at least 2 concrete example patterns
  ← Satisfies: "CLAUDE.md references sg as the preferred tool for structural code search"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Existing code in gate-2b-blast-radius.sh uses the ast-grep binary name; CLAUDE.md should clarify that sg and ast-grep are the same tool (sg is the CLI command name)
- [Reliability] Documentation should note that ast-grep is optional — all workflows function without it via Grep fallback

comments.body: Consideration: [Maintainability] The existing codebase (gate-2b-blast-radius.sh) uses ast-grep as the binary name, while the epic description and CLAUDE.md guidance will reference sg. Decide on one canonical name and document both as aliases if needed. The command -v guard must check whichever name is chosen consistently across all 7 files.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Done definition update: A standard guard snippet (command -v check + fallback pattern) is defined in the CLAUDE.md section so all downstream skill stories copy the same pattern verbatim rather than each inventing their own.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 6/6: Done ✓ — Files: CLAUDE.md (added Structural Code Search section with guard pattern, examples, binary name convention), README.md (added Optional Dependencies section with ast-grep install instructions). Tests: N/A (docs-only).
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Fixed: Added ast-grep installation instructions to README.md and structural code search section with canonical guard pattern and example patterns to CLAUDE.md
comments.author: Test
